steps.title: Set up the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle with legs $a$ and $b$ and hypotenuse $c$, we have $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$. With legs of 11 and 9 centimeters:
$$11^2 + 9^2 = c^2$$

steps.title: Calculate the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Square the legs and add:
$$121 + 81 = c^2$$
$$202 = c^2$$
$$c = \sqrt{202}$$
This matches the correct answer — B is correct.
